---
title: "Research Brief Generator"
subtitle: "LLM-assisted briefing workflow for health papers"
page-layout: article
toc: false
execute:
  echo: false
format:
  html:
    code-tools: false
    code-fold: false
    code-copy: false
---

```{=html}
<div style="padding:4.5rem 0 2.25rem;">
  <span class="section-eyebrow">Project C · LLM Application</span>
  <h1 class="section-h" style="font-size:clamp(2.2rem,4vw,3.2rem);margin-top:0.45rem;">Structured research briefs from long-form papers.</h1>
  <p class="section-sub" style="margin-bottom:0;">A lightweight AI application for turning dense research papers into a consistent briefing format that a programme lead, policymaker, or field operations team can read quickly.</p>
</div>
```

## Use case

Research papers often contain the right evidence but arrive in the wrong format for a busy delivery team. This project creates a structured bridge:

- input a paper as text or extracted PDF content
- ask the model for a fixed briefing schema
- export a short brief in JSON and markdown-ready text

## Brief schema

| Field | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `title` | Human-readable title |
| `research_question` | What the paper is trying to answer |
| `methods` | Study design in plain language |
| `main_findings` | Bullet-ready evidence summary |
| `operational_implications` | What a programme team should do with it |
| `equity_notes` | Any fairness or implementation concerns |
| `limitations` | What not to over-claim |

## Workflow

```{=html}
<div class="cv-stat-grid" style="margin-bottom:2rem;">
  <article class="cv-stat-card"><span class="cv-stat-num">1</span><span class="cv-stat-label">Load paper text</span></article>
  <article class="cv-stat-card"><span class="cv-stat-num">2</span><span class="cv-stat-label">Apply structured prompt</span></article>
  <article class="cv-stat-card"><span class="cv-stat-num">3</span><span class="cv-stat-label">Validate JSON output</span></article>
  <article class="cv-stat-card"><span class="cv-stat-num">4</span><span class="cv-stat-label">Publish briefing note</span></article>
</div>
```

## Files in this project

- [generate_brief.py](./generate_brief.py) for the CLI workflow
- [examples/sample_paper.txt](./examples/sample_paper.txt) as example input
- [examples/sample_brief.json](./examples/sample_brief.json) as example output
- [README.md](./README.md) with setup notes

## Why it matters

This project shows applied AI in a way that fits my background. It is not novelty for its own sake. It solves a real evidence translation problem and keeps the human reviewer in the loop.

[Back to Projects →](../../index.qmd){.btn-solid style="margin-top:2rem;display:inline-block;"}
